title: oreobatus deliciosus subsp. neomexicanus
intro: Oreobatus deliciosus subsp. neomexicanus, also known as New Mexican mountain mahonia, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States, specifically in New Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion, due to its saponins and alkaloids content. Herbal preparations of Oreobatus deliciosus subsp. neomexicanus can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by using the roots and bark to create a tincture.
